key_insight: MISTnetX learns a direct bridge from atomistic shock physics to continuum-scale response, enabling microstructure-aware prediction of shock-to-detonation transition in energetic composites.
engineering_relevance: This provides a parameter-free way to inform FE simulations with sub-grid hotspot and chemistry effects, improving predictive design of safer, more reliable energetic materials.
method: Train a convolutional deep neural network on large-scale MD shock simulations of complex microstructures.
Use the learned model to transfer shock-microstructure interaction, hotspot formation, and deflagration onset into FE models.
Couple mechanics, shock, thermal transport, and chemistry across scales without relying on classical scale-separation assumptions.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports application to a synthetic yet realistic nanostructured plastic-bonded RDX composite, where the trained model enables full run-to-detonation prediction.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the representativeness of the MD training set and on how well the synthetic microstructures capture real manufacturing variability.
paper_title: Microstructure-Aware Deep Learning Bridges Atomistics to Macroscale for Shock-to-Detonation Prediction
